title: Error in date parse from log file, it is not possible to see the container logs
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I can't see the logs from the jellyfin container, when I run 
```bash
podman logs --tail=100 jellyfin
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a jellyfin container (that example is exactly what I execute to create my container)
```bash
podman create \
  --name=jellyfin \
  --privileged \
  --restart=always \
  -e TZ=America/Sao_Paulo \
  -p 8096:8096 \
  -v /opt/data/Vídeos/jellyfin/jellyfin:/config \
  -v /opt//data/Vídeos/jellyfin/tvShows:/data/tvshows \
  -v /opt//data/Vídeos/jellyfin/Filmes:/data/movies \
  linuxserver/jellyfin
```
2. Run it
```bash
podman start jellyfin
```
3. Try to get the logs
```bash
podman logs --tail=100 jellyfin
```

**Describe the results you received:**

I received that output when I try do see the log, seems to be a error with a parse from the timestamp ?
```bash
Error: unable to read log file 91fdd32c59e9d4376e49c389e92093f74bafdf04b624271e89dc229fef6cb328 for /var/home/emerson/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/91fdd32c59e9d4376e49c389e92093f74bafdf04b624271e89dc229fef6cb328/userdata/ctr.log : unable to convert time  from container log: parsing time "" as "2006-01-02T15:04:05.000000000Z07:00": cannot parse "" as "2006"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

If I tail the file descripted on the command I can see the log, I guess this is what I need to see when running the podman logs command
```bash
tail -f /var/home/emerson/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers
```
output
```bash
/91fdd32c59e9d4376e49c389e92093f74bafdf04b624271e89dc229fef6cb328/userdata/ctr.log
2020-04-04T21:00:30.447079981-03:00 stdout F [21:00:30] [INF] [1] App: Executed all post-startup entry points in 0:00:00.2608729
2020-04-04T21:00:30.447106641-03:00 stdout F [21:00:30] [INF] [1] Main: Startup complete 0:00:04.5772437
2020-04-04T21:00:30.645704778-03:00 stdout F [21:00:30] [INF] [10] LibraryMonitor: Watching directory /data/movies

2020-04-04T21:00:31.012889394-03:00 stdout F [21:00:31] [INF] [6] LibraryMonitor: Watching directory /data/tvshows/Animes
2020-04-04T21:00:32.904564251-03:00 stdout F [21:00:32] [INF] [14] TaskManager: StartupTrigger fired for task: Update Plugins
2020-04-04T21:00:32.905100373-03:00 stdout F [21:00:32] [INF] [14] TaskManager: Queueing task PluginUpdateTask
2020-04-04T21:00:32.907051132-03:00 stdout F [21:00:32] [INF] [12] TaskManager: Executing Update Plugins
2020-04-04T21:00:34.030666731-03:00 stdout F [21:00:34] [INF] [21] TaskManager: Update Plugins Completed after 0 minute(s) and 1 seconds
2020-04-04T21:00:34.060047647-03:00 stdout F [21:00:34] [INF] [21] TaskManager: ExecuteQueuedTasks
```

```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
